worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's empirical token-count comparison between Opus 4.7 and 4.6 is the most concrete cost analysis in the feed — 1.46x text, up to 3x images at same per-token price.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Critical follow-up clarifying the 3x image cost spike is resolution-dependent, not universal — changes the pricing calculus for most use cases.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Ethan Mollick's counterfactual — OpenAI could have kept reasoners secret and delayed the DeepSeek moment — is a sharp strategic lens on open vs. closed AI release decisions.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Grok Build + Grok Computer merging into a unified desktop app is a meaningful competitive move in the agentic coding tool space worth tracking.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Claude Code being actively used at Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI internally, with a breakdown of casual vs. orchestration-level usage, is a real signal for product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Concrete report of Opus 4.7 confidently giving wrong answers and forcing a rollback to 4.6 — directly relevant to anyone evaluating the model for production agentic use.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The '30-day roadmap' take is blunt but high-signal for indie devs thinking about AI-era product velocity — 87K views suggests it's landing.

voices.why: Only voice in the feed doing actual empirical work on Opus 4.7 pricing — directly actionable for API cost planning.
Brings historical and strategic framing to AI model releases that's useful for product positioning thinking.
Sharp, contrarian takes on agent-native product strategy and distribution that cut through the hype.

examples: aakashgupta posting unrelated viral trivia (tennis longevity, NASA capsules, Nolan film, bones biology)
fuckgrowth affiliate marketing / crypto UGC hustle content
RoundtableSpace crypto shilling (AAVE exploit, Polymarket trading bots presented as AI demos)
